rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce new architecture elements.
Resolve type mismatches by ensuring expressions match the expected types (bool, int, real) and avoid <error> types in guarantees.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes; move valid bindings to implementation annexes.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference undefined or non-visible identifiers instead of attempting to define them.
generic_rules: AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and use only declared or visible identifiers.
Component type contracts should reference only features available at that scope; implementation bindings go in implementation scopes.
When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references, trace the root cause and correct or remove the offending clause.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers unless they are present in the model's configured dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with valid syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, assign.
Assign statements in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Helper declarations like `eq` and `const` should use correct scalar types: int, real, bool.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are resolvable within the current AADL component scope.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to define or resolve missing identifiers by adding new architecture elements or imports.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; this causes scope resolution errors.
Do not assume that AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) are automatically available as AGREE variables.
Do not mix package-qualified references with missing dependencies; this leads to unresolved reference errors.
